Document DSP voice coefficient routing subsystem

Add comprehensive documentation of the DSP coefficient routing
functions discovered during subcpu disassembly:

- Voice coefficient routing algorithm (type dispatch, index computation)
- Memory layout for routing tables (0x41368, 0x45210, 0x45310/45314)
- Routing configuration structure offsets (0x44-0x98)
- 7 newly-identified routing functions with descriptions
- DSP register write protocol (address/data via 0x130000)
- Prevbank register usage for 8-byte parameter passing
- Utility functions (block copy, fill, checksum)

chore: move allowed-tools permissions out of project settings (#1079)

All allow/ask rules moved to ~/.claude/settings.json (global, not
checked in). Project settings.json now contains only the deny guardrails,
hooks, enabledPlugins, and outputStyle — nothing that belongs in git.

Improvements made during migration:
- Bash(bd:*) replaces 20+ individual bd subcommand entries
- Bash(bash scripts/workflow/:*) consolidates 8 per-script entries
- bd sync:* and pinpoint-wt.py sync:* removed (stale commands)
- curl, pkill, docker volume rm moved allow→ask (match project intent)
- mcp__github__issue_write moved allow→ask
- settings.local.json stripped to remote ID + prefersReducedMotion only
- Global deny expanded with union of project deny rules

commit.message:
fix(pdf): sanitize <style> element text in onclone to eliminate lab/oklch errors

html2canvas parses raw <style> element text directly — our previous fix only
converted computed inline styles but left oklch/lab/lch values in the stylesheet
text itself. Added replaceColorsInCssText() which runs regex replacements across
all <style> elements in the cloned document before rendering, so no modern CSS
color function survives into the canvas.

commit.message:
Fix reminder: detect WhatsApp logout vs stale socket, alert via notification

- whatsapp_status() now returns 'connected' | 'stale' | 'logged_out'
  by checking for 'stopped' keyword in channel status output
- logged_out → immediate abort + macOS notification with re-link command;
  no wasted retries (previous behaviour: 3x90s of pointless waiting)
- stale → wait 45s for health-monitor, then continue retrying as before
- send_message() returns None on hard-logout error string to signal abort
- All failure paths fire osascript notification so Raju sees it on-screen

Root cause of Mar 6 miss: WhatsApp session was kicked (401) before 5PM;
script exhausted all retries silently with no alert to operator.

commit.message:
Fix manual mark time to interpret input as Eastern time

Previously the time input was interpreted in the browser's local
timezone. Now it correctly treats the input as Eastern (EST/EDT)
regardless of the browser's timezone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Support both User Journey and Technical Architecture scripts in Studio Mode

**Problem:** Studio Mode only supported User Journey scripts (hook, problem, solution, impact, cta) but users can also have Technical Architecture scripts with different chapters (context, logicGate, execution, moat).

**User Impact:** Users with Technical Architecture scripts saw only chapter titles ("I. The Context") but no content.

**Fix:** Added script type detection and conditional chapter mapping:

**Technical Architecture Script:**
- I. The Context (~50s)
- II. The Logic Gate (~80s)
- III. The Execution (~70s)
- IV. The Moat (~50s)

**User Journey Script:**
- 1. Hook (~15s)
- 2. Problem (~30s)
- 3. Solution (~60s)
- 4. Impact (~45s)
- 5. Call to Action (~15s)

**Code Changes:**
- StudioMode: Detects script.type and maps chapters accordingly
- Studio page: Passes full parsed script object with type preserved
- Both script types now display full content in teleprompter

Users can now use Studio Mode with either script type for frictionless recording.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: use node --import tsx/esm for PM2 to fix ESM-only package resolution

yahoo-finance2 only exports ESM (no CJS fallback). Using tsx as a PM2
interpreter triggers CJS resolution which fails. Switch to node with
--import tsx/esm loader for proper ESM support.

Add workflow hints to break Gemini's list_tabs loop

Gemini gets stuck calling list_tabs repeatedly without progressing.
Added [HINT] text after navigate and list_tabs responses to guide the

connect_bridge description to say auto-connect handles local Chrome.
Updated list_tabs description to say "only call ONCE".

commit.message: Fix cassette swapable layers: use instead of CSS background-image SVGs with internal url(#id) refs (gradients, filters) silently fail when used as CSS background-image in Chrome — the browser resolves url(#id) against the page document instead of the SVG file. Fix: replace the single ct-swapable div (CSS multi-layer background-image) with stacked elements. Each loads the SVG as its own document where url(#id) resolves correctly. Positioning math is identical. Also documents the full local asset pipeline in CLAUDE.md. Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6

commit.message:
Chain FFN ops on device + trace capture for all matmuls (~255ms/tok)

- Chain gate_up matmul → slice → SiLU → multiply → down matmul on device,
  eliminating PCIe round-trip between FFN matmuls
- Pre-allocate intermediate FFN buffers (gate, up, act) on device
- Add trace capture/replay for all 65 matmul calls + 32 FFN chains,
  reducing host-side dispatch overhead from ~0.7ms to ~0.09ms per call
- Profile breakdown per device_gemv: enqueue=0.55ms, dispatch=0.09ms,
  read=1.23ms (blocking sync dominates)
- Performance: ~255ms/tok (3.9 tok/s), improved from ~290ms/tok (3.4 tok/s)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve build failures on Media-Server, Radxa-X4, and multi-host limits

Media-Server:
- Add lib.mkForce to immich UMask overrides — upstream now also sets
  UMask = "0077", causing a conflict without explicit priority

Radxa-X4 (vpn-qbittorrent):
- Escape ${WG_DNS:-1.1.1.1} as ''${...} in writeShellScript — Nix was
  parsing WG_DNS:-1.1.1.1 as a lambda expression, breaking evaluation

Nix build job limits (Ryzen5900x, Ryzen5800x, Asus-A15, ThinkPad):
- Cap max-jobs to numThreads/4 on each host to prevent OOM freezes
  when compiling large packages (LLVM, ROCm, Chromium) in parallel

Fish aliases:
- Add --print-build-logs to all nixos-rebuild aliases on both
  config.fish and config-server.fish for better build visibility

Flake:
- Bump nixpkgs to aca4d95 (2026-03-05)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(explain): add EXPLAIN mode selector (Plan, Pipeline, AST, Syntax, Estimate) (#824)

* feat(explain): add EXPLAIN mode selector (Plan, Pipeline, AST, Syntax, Estimate)

Add mode tabs to the Explain Query page, unlocking ClickHouse's full
EXPLAIN capabilities. Users can now switch between Plan, Pipeline, AST,
Syntax, and Estimate modes to get different views of query execution.

- API: accept `mode` query param with validation against allowlist
- UI: add Tabs component for mode selection above the query input
- Result card title updates to reflect selected mode

Co-Authored-By: duyetbot <duyetbot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(explain): remove unreachable PLAN from VALID_EXPLAIN_MODES

The UI Plan tab sends mode='' (empty string) which maps to plain EXPLAIN
(equivalent to EXPLAIN PLAN). Having 'PLAN' in the server allowlist was
unreachable and caused an out-of-sync allowlist between API and UI.

Removing 'PLAN' keeps both sides consistent: empty = default EXPLAIN PLAN,
non-empty values are explicit modes (PIPELINE, AST, SYNTAX, ESTIMATE).

Co-Authored-By: duyetbot <duyetbot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: exclude .claude/worktrees from biome and git checks

Worktree directories contain their own biome.json files which cause
biome to fail with "nested root configuration" errors during pre-push.
Adding to .gitignore excludes them from biome's file traversal
(via vcs.useIgnoreFile: true) and prevents accidental commits.

Co-Authored-By: duyetbot <duyetbot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

The Jacquard Thread (#42) — How LLMs went from sentences to software

220-year thread from Jacquard's silk loom punch cards to modern AI agents
writing code. Teaches real computing history (Jacquard, Babbage, Lovelace,
Hopper) AND installs the Jacquard Principle: every revolution happens when
the pattern separates from the practitioner.

7 chapters, visual experience with animated SVG loom, timeline, capability
charts, comparison matrices, pyramid hierarchy, concept maps. Technology domain.
